title: GitHub Profile, Repository Health, and Verifiable Work · Opportunity Brief: Repository Health
summary: A profile has a dense contribution graph and many forked tutorials, but reviewers cannot find three relevant, runnable, maintained, or honestly attributed examples. Predict the hiring decision, evidence quality, affected people, uncertainty, and ethical boundary before reviewing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the repository health increment in selective GitHub evidence route. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence…
objectives: Show license, contribution, conduct, support, security, issue, dependency, release, and ownership signals appropriate to the project.
Create a fast repository entry path through purpose, users, status, demonstration, setup, test, architecture, and limitation evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Predict Repository Health · Theory Repository Health
steps.instruction:
theory case — A dense contribution graph and ten pinned tutorials hide the three relevant projects, setup path, tests, current status, contribution boundary, and limitations. The theory variation 4 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: defend one bounded career decision

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Repository Health contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Repository Health · Theory Repository Health.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for show license, contribution, conduct, support, security, issue, dependency, release, and ownership signals appropriate to the project. For Predict Repository Health · Theory Repository Health, keep Repository Health connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A dense contribution graph and ten pinned tutorials hide the three relevant projects, setup path, tests, current status, contribution boundary, and limitations. The theory variation 4 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: defend one bounded career decision

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Repository Health condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: O*NET Employer-Based In-Demand Software Skills — Supports time- and location-bounded technology demand comparisons while prohibiting claims that posting frequency equals job importance or learner fit.

At Predict Repository Health · Theory Repository Health, trace this concrete input and predict targeting, source, evidence, contribution, access, privacy, consent, process, decision, rejection, repair, follow-up, and learner-controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: A dense contribution graph and ten pinned tutorials hide the three relevant projects, setup path, tests, current status, contribution boundary, and limitations. The theory variation 4 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: defend one bounded career decision

For Predict Repository Health · Theory Repository Health, challenge the documented misconception with changed-candidate, target-role, source-date, jurisdiction, audience, access, privacy, consent, process-state, interview, offer, rejection, repair, and learner-controlled transfer evidence: Repository community files are decorative for solo work.

options.text: Show license, contribution, conduct, support, security, issue, dependency, release, and ownership signals appropriate to the project. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.
Repository community files are decorative for solo work. That claim fails the predict repository health evidence boundary for Repository Health.

For predict repository health, apply Repository Health without its stated preconditions, omit the selective GitHub evidence route trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Repository Health for selective GitHub evidence route.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, changed candidate, rejected failure, causal repair, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer limit; then calibrate the application or career decision to that exact evidence. Apply that move specifically to Repository Health while producing selective GitHub evidence route.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A dense contribution graph and ten pinned tutorials hide the three relevant projects, setup path, tests, current status, contribution boundary, and limitations. The worked example variation 4 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Show license, contribution, conduct, support, security, issue, dependency, release, and ownership signals appropriate to the project. A defensible solution applies Repository Health, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to selective GitHub evidence route.

content.code:
# Worked focus: career-repository-health — Show license, contribution, conduct, support, security, issue, dependency, release, and ownership signals appropriate to the project.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-candidate-4
focus: career-repository-health
decision-evidence: Show license, contribution, conduct, support, security, issue, dependency, release, and ownership signals appropriate to the project.
target-role: accessible software quality engineer
jurisdiction: example location requiring current official review
audience: technical reviewer with ten minutes and keyboard-only access
claim: one bounded role behavior is supported by a revision-linked changed case
evidence: repository case study, exact test result, contribution note, and limitation
process-state: investigate before applying
accessibility: structured text, reflow, visible focus, plain-language status, and equivalent format
privacy-consent: publish no sensitive person or third-party data; contact only with explicit consent
decision: proceed only after identity, role, evidence, access, and risk checks pass
verify: inspect links, extraction order, revision, changed case, disclosure, and source date
failure: reject invented impact, hidden assistance, exposed data, or unverified recruiter identity
repair: correct the first unsupported claim and add a changed-candidate regression
owner: learner controls every external action
transfer-boundary: manual learner-approved profile, application, conversation, interview, and offer decisions
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Repository community files are decorative for solo work. This fails the Repository Health contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: O*NET Employer-Based In-Demand Software Skills (U.S. postings from 2025; reviewed 2026-07-15) — Supports time- and location-bounded technology demand comparisons while prohibiting claims that posting frequency equals job importance or learner fit.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Show license, contribution, conduct, support, security, issue, dependency, release, and ownership signals appropriate to the project.
